## **Review Themes** {#review-themes}

### Forest: Focus for Productivity {#review-app-1}

   **Bugs and stability.**
      22 recent review(s) mention bugs and stability.
      Review window: Feb 24, 2026 - Jul 19, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users report reliability problems. This points to quality gaps competitors can exploit.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _Mukul Gupta_, Jul 17, 2026, 1/5: "Overtime this has only got more buggy and laggy. Not what I expected after buying the premium subscription."
            2. _eywu_, Jul 9, 2026, 1/5: "I updated the app, and completely forgot my old account. It asks me to sign up again. When inputting all my information and verifying my email, I continually get an error message that says “Oops! Something went wrong. Please try again.” All that time and money, lost to a broken app."
            3. _Lildvxgkskgd_, Jul 9, 2026, 1/5: "The app keeps crashing after the latest update. It won't even open—as soon as I launch it, it immediately kicks me out. I tried uninstalling and reinstalling, but the issue persists. Please fix this bug!"

   **Pricing and paywall.**
      19 recent review(s) mention pricing and paywall.
      Review window: Feb 10, 2026 - Jul 19, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users mention price, subscriptions, or paywalls. This can signal pricing sensitivity in the niche.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _w y_, Jul 9, 2026, 1/5: "paid for Forest plus and still got ads (not the optional ones for extra rewards)"
            2. _hyjdidnfb_, Jun 10, 2026, 1/5: "i liked the app but that you paywall something that i initially payey for is so greedy. i want to see my tags and pie charts as an overview. why do i see advertisement in an app i payed for!!!"
            3. _Lumie__, Apr 24, 2026, 1/5: "I paid for the app years ago, now theyve added a subscription plan on top. App is full of upselling and bloat now. I paid for the entire app, i paid for a clean experience, now theyre trying to milk as i money as possible from their remaining userbase. Greedy developers, please do not buy or support"

   **Ads pressure.**
      5 recent review(s) mention ads pressure.
      Review window: Jun 23, 2026 - Jul 13, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users are noticing ad pressure. This can signal monetization friction and an opening for a cleaner competitor.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _w y_, Jul 9, 2026, 1/5: "paid for Forest plus and still got ads (not the optional ones for extra rewards)"
            2. _TheReviewersreview_, Jun 23, 2026, 1/5: "This app sucks now because you guys putting too many ads!"
            3. _I just wanna read da Bible_, Jul 13, 2026, 3/5: "I really love this app (that I bought years ago), and as a graduate student I used to use it frequently. Recently, I needed a way to re-balance my time, and thought to re-download. Now it’s loaded with tons of ads and requests to purchase premium. It still works, but it’s a bit much and is irksome to use especially considering I paid for it (eg unskippable ad after most sessions?)"

   **Notifications and reminders.**
      4 recent review(s) mention notifications and reminders.
      Review window: Jul 14, 2026 - Jul 19, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users mention reminders or notifications. In productivity niches, this often points to retention mechanics.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _Antony Filyk_, Jul 19, 2026, 2/5: "They've added subscription-based monetisation after I already purchased premium. Also, there is this annoying red dot notification that doesn't go away no matter what you do. The UI is buggy, for the subscription based service with heavy in-app monetisation this is a really bad business practice. I don't like how this app that supposedly should help battle distractions becomes the distraction itself."
            2. _Arjun Singhan Patil_, Jul 14, 2026, 3/5: "I'm not satisfied with this version, it manipulates the time , sometimes it increases or reduces actual. Its notification after getting disconnected or reconnected the internet is a little annoying. please improve"
---

### Google Kalender {#review-app-2}

   **Bugs and stability.**
      9 recent review(s) mention bugs and stability.
      Review window: Jul 8, 2026 - Jul 18, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users report reliability problems. This points to quality gaps competitors can exploit.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _Cid Gomez_, Jul 18, 2026, 1/5: "Keeps crashing, cannot launch it through any way - icon press, play store, etc. Redmagic 11 Pro+"
            2. _Federico Massari_, Jul 9, 2026, 2/5: "Various bugs such as the app not responding and exiting automatically, inability to change, add or delete events, inability to change event color (default color only), clearing the Cache doesn't solve the problem, reinstalling doesn't even work. From redmagic 11 pro"
            3. _A. T._, Jul 9, 2026, 2/5: "For two days now I have only been able to choose the default color when I want to put an appointment in my calendar, while I often use multiple colors for a good overview. All new appointments now have the same color. How can I fix this? Or is there a bug in the new update? Had another update on July 9 and the problem is still not solved..."

   **Feature requests.**
      6 recent review(s) mention feature requests.
      Review window: Jul 18, 2026 - Jul 19, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users are asking for missing capabilities. This can reveal roadmap opportunities.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _Johnny Mears_, Jul 19, 2026, 1/5: "I can't add a public calendar from the app??? I have to use a web browser??? That seems like such a basic feature. How is this not available in the app??? The calendar app has been around for years! Did Google layoff all the calendar app devs to invest in AI? I bet Gemini could add that feature in an afternoon! This is why Google sucks! They rather shove AI in your face than focus on features people actually use."
            2. _Daniel Oliveira_, Jul 19, 2026, 5/5: "Needs more colors for main scheduling notes, not just the subnotes. Lastly, it needs an easy go to certain date feature. I don't know why this hasn't been though of? It's so elementary. It's difficult to go to a far future or past date."
            3. _Syed Ali Mehdi Zaidi_, Jul 18, 2026, 5/5: "​"Great app, but really needs two essential updates for Hijri Calendar users: 1. Option for manual Hijri date adjustment (+/- 1 or 2 days) for regional moon sightings. 2. A clean lock screen widget showing both Gregorian and Hijri dates. These additions would be extremely helpful for millions of users worldwide.""

   **Sync and accounts.**
      3 recent review(s) mention sync and accounts.
      Review window: Jul 18, 2026 - Jul 18, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users mention sync, backup, login, or accounts. This points to trust and cross-device workflow expectations.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _Pink Babe_, Jul 18, 2026, 1/5: "STILL NEEDS IMPROVEMENT. STILL cant change widget color or transparency like samsung calendar. Didn't sync all events from samsung calendar. And the colors are dull and ugly. Should be brighter. I also dont want to see the next months days when looking at the current month. why would u show 5 or 6 weeks at a time? nobody does that. and its confusing. it also stretches it out too much and looks silly. still be using samsung calendar til they improve this one, 3 years and still no change."

   **Notifications and reminders.**
      3 recent review(s) mention notifications and reminders.
      Review window: Jul 18, 2026 - Jul 18, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users mention reminders or notifications. In productivity niches, this often points to retention mechanics.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _FERNANDAS GEORGE_, Jul 18, 2026, 4/5: "Need Reminder Alaram"

### Notion: Ant, Uppg, AI {#review-app-3}

   **Bugs and stability.**
      19 recent review(s) mention bugs and stability.
      Review window: May 6, 2026 - Jul 18, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users report reliability problems. This points to quality gaps competitors can exploit.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _J A_, Jul 18, 2026, 1/5: "Unsuable. Copying note from Windows app to Word 1) loses all formatting, inserting weird symbols instead 2) inserts all text as list items. Also bugs 1. if there are no pages inside, expand icon should not be shown 2. removing text from beginning of the page should not switch to page header and remove that text as well. And a bunch of bugs in Windows app"
            2. _Stefano Polese_, May 6, 2026, 1/5: "Notion Is Amazing, the app is terrible. Random bugs pop up constantly, I couldn't access my teamspace for weeks, then it erases part of what I'm writing while I'm writing it and sometimes it doesn't load any page, absurd."
            3. _Moesha Njoroge_, May 31, 2026, 2/5: "App used to be good, even though you have to switch to browser for some things. But it just keeps getting worse - crashing, lagging, not uploading pictures correctly. It's obvious that they don't care about their mobile users, no matter if you use the app or browser."

   **Feature requests.**
      3 recent review(s) mention feature requests.
      Review window: Jul 18, 2026 - Jul 18, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users are asking for missing capabilities. This can reveal roadmap opportunities.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _brah why you do this_, Jul 18, 2026, 2/5: "I like the app, but i really wish i could disable all the ai features, I don't want to even see them. it's super annoying."

### Tiimo: Daily To Do List {#review-app-4}

   **Bugs and stability.**
      8 recent review(s) mention bugs and stability.
      Review window: Jun 10, 2026 - Jul 14, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users report reliability problems. This points to quality gaps competitors can exploit.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _Glitter Blood_, Jul 14, 2026, 3/5: "Useful app which definitely helps with my adhd task initiation/keeping track, so lots of potential but it is quite buggy - the latest update has meant I can no longer move one days tasks to the next or to a different day - when I click them to move it nothing happens. Also annoying that if you don’t want to schedule specific times to do each task on the daily view tab, you can’t reorder them. Trying to set times is impractical and time consuming unless it’s a specific thing like a meeting, ideally you’d be able to create high to low priority on the day view, or at least reorder them easily so you can see which order you need do the tasks in - every time I try to reorder, even if I seem to successfully move a task up or down, it puts it back to where it was before if I click off that page or close the app. Would also be helpful if completed tasks automatically delete after 30 days or something on the main prioritisation lists."
            2. _Alea-pea_, Jun 23, 2026, 3/5: "I really love this app and it has helped me immensely when it comes to getting organised– although it’s an ongoing process and I’m still learning! If this app worked the way it was intended I would have given it five stars however it often glitches on my iPhone laptop and iPad. These glitches can be very frustrating. I look forward to any improvements and to upgrading my rating to five stars. Many thanks for the app!"
            3. _chilliflakes_, Jul 14, 2026, 4/5: "I like the simple and intuitive UI of the app. It does feel much better than calendar and reminder systems I’ve used in the past already. However, this app is unusable offline, which for me is a shame as I cannot use it while commuting through rural areas (the time I plan my day). I would pay past the trial of it wasn’t for this."

   **Pricing and paywall.**
      5 recent review(s) mention pricing and paywall.
      Review window: May 25, 2026 - Jul 15, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users mention price, subscriptions, or paywalls. This can signal pricing sensitivity in the niche.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _Damonic_, Jul 15, 2026, 1/5: "I downloaded this app and tried it during the trial period. I canceled the subscription before I was supposed to be charged but they charged me anyway. They refuse to refund the money. Beware before you accept the trial period."
            2. _LatteGirl11_, Jul 1, 2026, 1/5: "I have been given marketed to get 30% off the annual rate and yet it’s the same as the most expensive rate advertised in the Apple App Store. Also, there are lower rates in the “In-App Purchases” section and yet I can’t access those rates. I can’t get a trial period and I can’t get a reduced rate to try to app so I guess I won’t try the app. The most expensive rate is too much to find out this is an app that doesn’t work for me. I’ve tried contacting the company multiple times instead of leaving a review but I have never received a response. Here are all the rates I see and yet the only one offered to me is the one for $54 even when I get a link to get 30% off of the annual rate. That is false advertising. In-App Purchases Tlimo Pro (1 Year) $54.00 Pro (1 Year) $48.00 Pro (1 Year) $54.00 Pro (1 Month) $12.00 Tiimo Pro (1 Month) $12.00 Pro (1 Year) $36.00 Pro (1 Month) $12.00 Pro (1 Month) $7.00 Pro (1 Year) $42.00 Pro (1 Month) $11.99"
            3. _Kathy Orebaugh_, Jun 14, 2026, 1/5: "I had a very unsatisfactory experience. I attempted to cancel my subscription on the final day of the free trial period, but the cancellation did not process successfully. After deleting the application, I made another attempt the following day. However, I was informed that a refund could not be issued due to their established refund policy. The application itself proved challenging to navigate, which ultimately led to my discontinuation of its use. I advise caution regarding their free trial."
---

### CamScanner - PDF-scanner app {#review-app-5}

   **Bugs and stability.**
      1 recent review(s) mention bugs and stability.
      Review window: Jul 7, 2026 - Jul 7, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users report reliability problems. This points to quality gaps competitors can exploit.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _Dean Eau_, Jul 7, 2026, 1/5: "used to be my favorite scanner, but now it is just annoying. After almost every scan there are ads, sometimes several in a row. The X buttons are often hard to see or disappear. One wrong tap sends you to the Play Store and you cannot return properly. Good scan quality, but ads and subscription prompts make it unusable for me."

   **Ads pressure.**
      6 recent review(s) mention ads pressure.
      Review window: Jun 22, 2026 - Jul 18, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users are noticing ad pressure. This can signal monetization friction and an opening for a cleaner competitor.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _Yoav Aviran Grad_, Jul 18, 2026, 1/5: "more ads then features"
            2. _R. Fra_, Jul 16, 2026, 1/5: "way too many ads that makes the use of the app a real hassle"
            3. _Ben No_, Jun 22, 2026, 1/5: "questionable ads everywhere. this used to be a good app but every year it got more and more adds. the content of the adds is very dubious to say the least. there are better apps out there that can do more and and add free incl. better privacy."

   **Pricing and paywall.**
      6 recent review(s) mention pricing and paywall.
      Review window: Jul 17, 2026 - Jul 18, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users mention price, subscriptions, or paywalls. This can signal pricing sensitivity in the niche.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _Sarah Burris_, Jul 18, 2026, 1/5: "Yes i still love what i could do with it. but i cancelled the trial period because i lost my job and they continuously keep trying to take $49.99 out of my account every month. THATS CALLED STEALING GUYS!!! STOP TRYING TO STEAL FROM ME. I CANCELLED IN PLENTY OF TIME AND YOU CANT DO THAT TO PEOPLE."
            2. _A Google user_, Jul 17, 2026, 2/5: "I've happily used this for years and paid for premium for a while. The base functionality is still just as good but all the ads are truly horrible and clicking the cross on them usually clicks through so that clearly doesn't work. The price is way too high and subscription based which is kinda ridiculous and I only use it twice a year. I get that they need to make money but this has gone too far now."
            3. _Jed Chan_, Jul 17, 2026, 3/5: "Update: Fr. 5 to 3 stars. I paid the one-time fee and used no other scanner since 2014. But slowly they move basic features like 'reordering pages' to their new premium plan. I now need to pay for something that was free. - I use this to scan and keep important documents which kept my office clutter free! Moreover, it also boosts my productivity by allowing me to sign contracts, scan, and send through email. Very convenient. No complains so far. Excited to see future developments for this app."

   **Sync and accounts.**
      2 recent review(s) mention sync and accounts.
      Review window: Jul 18, 2026 - Jul 18, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users mention sync, backup, login, or accounts. This points to trust and cross-device workflow expectations.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _Sarah Burris_, Jul 18, 2026, 1/5: "Yes i still love what i could do with it. but i cancelled the trial period because i lost my job and they continuously keep trying to take $49.99 out of my account every month. THATS CALLED STEALING GUYS!!! STOP TRYING TO STEAL FROM ME. I CANCELLED IN PLENTY OF TIME AND YOU CANT DO THAT TO PEOPLE."

### Opal: Screen Time Control {#review-app-7}

   **Bugs and stability.**
      22 recent review(s) mention bugs and stability.
      Review window: Jun 8, 2026 - Jul 15, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users report reliability problems. This points to quality gaps competitors can exploit.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _Wvoqfent_, Jun 26, 2026, 1/5: "After the latest update, the app is very slow and does not block the apps even when rules are active. Please fix it!"
            2. _machitivuole_, Jul 9, 2026, 2/5: "The last update has made this app so much worse than it was!! So many things were changed that didn’t need to be changed and it’s overall so glitchy now. Moreover the push notifications have become so annoying (and way too frequent). 😔😔😔"
            3. _Rezensionentipper_, Jul 2, 2026, 2/5: "I was using Opal for more than a year and it was fantastic! Now with the Update it is just lagging when trying to pause, draining battery and also not blocking properly. This is still going on, so unfortunately I have to remove the app. I would appreciate the version before the update or if all the issues are fixed but as of now, this is not the best UX. Good luck!"

   **Pricing and paywall.**
      5 recent review(s) mention pricing and paywall.
      Review window: Jun 22, 2026 - Jul 15, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users mention price, subscriptions, or paywalls. This can signal pricing sensitivity in the niche.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _aafjdbjtgnkiiloolklll_, Jul 15, 2026, 1/5: "App works great, but for 100€ a year the devs are greedy jews. Literally kill yourselves like wtf. I don’t know what degenerates actually pay for this. If it was 100€ to buy premium for life - i’d do it. BUT A SUBSCRIPTION MODEL 😭😭😭🙏🏻 Kys nigga wtf"
            2. _Moe8206_, Jun 26, 2026, 1/5: "SEE UPDATE BELOW PLS! Opal is one of the best desicions I made this year, it helps me concentrate on the important things (studying, work, sport, etc) in life and to end constant procrastination and distraction through social media. Even if you for once really need to use your social media accounts or just need a short break it offers useful short break timers. The app is highly costomizable which is a huge +. The only negative part is the unnecessary very high (yearly or monthly) subscription prize for the PRO version that offers even more features. 99€/ year for such an simple app is just a bit too much. In conclusion however I definitely recommend Opal to anyone that struggels with a high screen time. :) UPDATE JUNE 2026 After the most recent update the app is laggy and awfully unusable. Please change this!"
            3. _Alice.Ttt_, Jun 22, 2026, 2/5: "In the new version for IPhones rules don’t work for websites. That feature was important for me and I want to cancel the subscription"

### Google Keep – anteckn. & lista {#review-app-8}

   **Bugs and stability.**
      4 recent review(s) mention bugs and stability.
      Review window: Jun 27, 2026 - Jul 19, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users report reliability problems. This points to quality gaps competitors can exploit.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _Elio Guzman_, Jul 19, 2026, 1/5: "Smh, the widget to see reminders doesn't work. Fix the bugs and re-code the app again, please. Or send it to me, I'll fix it."
            2. _G_, Jun 27, 2026, 1/5: "update: typing has become unbearably slow to the point I can no longer use this app. When I go shopping with a checklist, I only want to be able to check things off, and the rest must be read only. This prevents the on-screen keyboard from coming up every time, creating ghost items, or accidentally archiving the list."
            3. _Luigi Domenico Massa_, Jul 10, 2026, 2/5: "after the latest updates the widget no longer updates in real time. The integration with tasks and calendar is a big mess: synchronization of the three often has bugs, changes in keep are not reported in tasks and calendar; a nice little house. But wasn't it better before everything was managed only by keep? They then removed the notification based on location; this was a very useful function: when I am in that place do you remind me to do this?; but why did you remove it?"

   **Sync and accounts.**
      2 recent review(s) mention sync and accounts.
      Review window: Jul 19, 2026 - Jul 19, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users mention sync, backup, login, or accounts. This points to trust and cross-device workflow expectations.

         **Review examples:**
            1. _Oliver Burling_, Jul 19, 2026, 2/5: "Works fine with Google home, but the app itself is very barebones, and can't do any of: 1. A transparent background, or colour of my choice 2. Sync to an app that can, like todoist 3. Easily check off items through the widget, meaning I need to click into the app Therefore, I'm better off just adding things manually to an app that can show them in the wya I want, rather than using keep with google home to be "smart" about it."

   **Feature requests.**
      1 recent review(s) mention feature requests.
      Review window: Jul 18, 2026 - Jul 18, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users are asking for missing capabilities. This can reveal roadmap opportunities.

### Google Tasks {#review-app-9}

   **Bugs and stability.**
      4 recent review(s) mention bugs and stability.
      Review window: Jun 20, 2026 - Jul 17, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users report reliability problems. This points to quality gaps competitors can exploit.

         **Review examples:**

            3. _Stefan Matthias Cornelius Glauninger_, Jun 20, 2026, 4/5: "Great app. Freezes often, unfortunately. Would like to add a sound when checking a done task."

      6 recent review(s) mention feature requests.
      Review window: Jul 17, 2026 - Jul 18, 2026
      **Why it matters:** Users are asking for missing capabilities. This can reveal roadmap opportunities.

### Microsoft To Do: Lists & Tasks {#review-app-10}

   **Bugs and stability.**
      8 recent review(s) mention bugs and stability.
      Review window: May 5, 2026 - Jul 15, 2026

## **Opportunities** {#opportunities}

1. Reliability as differentiation: Tiimo: Daily To Do List, Forest: Focus for Productivity, Notion: Ant, Uppg, AI, Microsoft OneNote: Save Notes — 111 reviews. Focus: win on stable core flows, crash-free updates, and careful regression testing.
2. Clearer pricing and value packaging: Forest: Focus for Productivity, Tiimo: Daily To Do List, Noji: Karteikarten Lernen, Todoist: to-dos och kalender — 39 reviews. Focus: make upgrade value explicit and avoid surprising existing paid users.
3. Roadmap ideas from unmet needs: Notion: Ant, Uppg, AI, Todoist: to-dos och kalender, Microsoft To Do: Lists & Tasks, Microsoft OneNote: Save Notes — 22 reviews. Focus: mine concrete requested workflows before adding broad feature surface.
4. Cleaner monetization: CamScanner - PDF-scanner app, Forest: Focus for Productivity, WPS Office-PDF, Word, Sheet — 10 reviews. Focus: reduce interruptions, separate paid clean mode, and avoid ads after purchase.
5. Trustworthy cross-device workflow: Google Kalender, CamScanner - PDF-scanner app, Google Keep – anteckn. & lista — 7 reviews. Focus: make login, backup, and sync predictable across devices.
6. Better notification control: Forest: Focus for Productivity, Google Kalender, Todoist: to-dos och kalender — 11 reviews. Focus: give users timing, frequency, and intensity controls.
